# @stapel/tokens 0.5.0

Neutral colour-role dictionary (§68; frontend-guardrails §1). Generated from
theme.default.json (or a host's stapel.theme.json); code is never hand-edited.
Theme switches via a `data-theme` attribute on the root — never a JS re-render,
never reading hex at runtime.

## The one right way (do this; anything else is a lint/review smell)
- Tailwind v4 (default adapter, static utilities — import `@stapel/tokens/tailwind.css`):
    <div class="bg-surface text-text border-border rounded p-4">
    <button class="bg-brand text-text-on-accent">Buy</button>
- Plain CSS: `color: var(--stapel-text)` · `background: var(--stapel-brand)`
- TS: `cssVar("brand")` → `var(--stapel-brand)` (typed; typos fail to compile)
- Tailwind v3 (legacy): import `@stapel/tokens/tailwind-v3.css` + spread
  `@stapel/tokens/tailwind-v3.config.cjs` into `theme.extend.colors`.

## Never (closed by construction and/or lint)
- hex/rgb/hsl literals in components — hex is born ONLY in ramps (theme.json).
- raw ramps (`gray.500`, `@stapel/tokens/raw`) in components — theme-config/showcase only;
  raw ramps are NOT CSS vars, there is no `--stapel-raw-*` to reference.
- Tailwind arbitrary values with interpolation (`bg-[${x}]`) — JIT cannot see them.

## Levels
L1 ramps (raw hex) → role (semantic, {light,dark} pair, `--stapel-<role>`).
A role missing a pair is a BUILD ERROR.

## Roles (`--stapel-<role>`, Tailwind `*-<role>`)
border, border-subtle, brand, brand-active, brand-hover, brand-subtle, error, error-bg, error-border, error-on, focus-ring, info, info-bg, info-border, info-on, link, link-hover, success, success-bg, success-border, success-on, surface, surface-overlay, surface-raised, surface-sunken, text, text-muted, text-on-accent, text-subtle, warning, warning-bg, warning-border, warning-on

## Non-colour scales (`--stapel-space-*`, `-radius-*`, `-font-size-*`, `-elevation-*`)
spacing: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 · radii: none, sm, md, lg, xl, full
fontSize: xs, sm, md, lg, xl, 2xl, 3xl · fontWeight: regular, medium, semibold, bold
